metadata.description: Second, independent set of realistic evaluation cases for the Technion Academic Advisor agent system. Ground-truth answers are derived exclusively from the wiki knowledge base at catalog_valut/wiki/, verified directly against the source files at authoring time. Deliberately avoids overlap with eval_cases.json's topics (e.g. uses the Economics minor instead of the already-covered Robotics minor).

cases.user_request: What are the prerequisites for Operating Systems (course 02340123), and which tracks require it?

correct_answer.summary: Operating Systems (02340123, 4.5 credits) requires Computer Organization and Programming (02340118) and Introduction to Systems Programming (02340124) as prerequisites. It is required in nine tracks, including Computer Engineering, Software Engineering, and several Computer Science concentrations.
key_facts: Course code: 02340123
Credits: 4.5
Prerequisite 1: 02340118 — Computer Organization and Programming
Prerequisite 2: 02340124 — Introduction to Systems Programming
Required in track: track-computer-engineering
Required in track: track-computer-science-bioinformatics
Required in track: track-computer-science-cyber
Required in track: track-computer-science-data-ml
Required in track: track-computer-science-general-4year
Required in track: track-cs-mathematics
Required in track: track-cs-medicine-dual
Required in track: track-cs-physics
Required in track: track-software-engineering

cases.evaluation_notes: The agent must name both prerequisite courses (by code and name) and should mention several of the nine required-in tracks rather than just one. A common failure mode is listing only one prerequisite or confusing this course's own code with a prerequisite's code.

cases.user_request: If I want to appeal an exam grade at Technion, how long do I have to submit the appeal, and what can happen as a result?

correct_answer.summary: A grade appeal must be submitted within 4 days from when the exam copy becomes available. The lecturer must respond within 1 week, and no later than 1 week before Moed B. The result of an appeal may raise, lower, or leave the grade unchanged, and the entire exam may be re-graded, not just the appealed question.
key_facts: Appeal deadline: within 4 days from when the exam copy is available
Lecturer response deadline: within 1 week, no later than 1 week before Moed B
An appeal may raise, lower, or leave the grade unchanged
The entire exam may be re-graded, not just the appealed question
If reserve duty was served during the appeal period, the deadline is extended by the number of days served
Source regulation: Section 5.4 / תקנה 2.3.3

cases.evaluation_notes: The agent must warn that an appeal can lower the grade, not just raise it — this is the most commonly missed fact. A correct answer should not imply the appeal is risk-free.

cases.user_request: I did 10 consecutive days of reserve duty in the 3 weeks before my exam period starts. Am I entitled to extra time on my exams, and how much?

correct_answer.summary: Yes — the student is entitled to 25% extra time. The regulation grants 25% extra exam time to a student who serves at least 10 continuous days of reserve duty in the 4 weeks before the exam period, and 10 consecutive days within 3 weeks satisfies that threshold.
key_facts: 25% extra time applies if a student serves at least 10 continuous days of reserve duty in the 4 weeks before the exam period
10 consecutive days within 3 weeks before the exam period satisfies this threshold
The extra-time entitlement is 25%
25% extra time is also granted for at least 14 cumulative days during the semester, or at least 10 days during the exam period itself (alternate qualifying conditions)
Source regulation: Section 5.10 / תקנה 2.4

cases.evaluation_notes: Tests whether the agent can correctly match a specific scenario against multiple alternative regulation thresholds (this case satisfies the '10 continuous days in the 4 weeks before the exam period' clause specifically). A wrong answer might claim no entitlement, or cite the wrong threshold/percentage.

cases.user_request: I'm considering the Economics minor. What GPA and credit requirements do I need to meet, and how many extra credits does the minor itself require beyond my home track?

correct_answer.summary: To register for the Economics minor, a student needs a cumulative GPA of 80 or higher and at least 36 accumulated credit points in their home program. The minor itself requires at least 7 additional credits beyond home-track requirements, and it is open to B.Sc. students from any Technion faculty.
key_facts: Admission GPA requirement: 80 or higher
Admission credit requirement: at least 36 credit points accumulated in the home program
Minimum extra credits for the minor itself: at least 7 credits beyond home track requirements
Available to all Technion B.Sc. students, from any faculty
Formal registration: submit student request form to the DDS faculty secretariat
Students may begin taking minor courses before formal registration

cases.evaluation_notes: Distinct from the existing golden set's Robotics-minor case — tests whether the agent retrieves the correct minor's page rather than confusing it with Robotics (GPA 87, 60 credits, 27 extra credits) due to structural similarity between the two minors' pages.

cases.user_request: How many total credits does the Cyber & Systems Security concentration in Computer Science require, and how many of those are faculty electives?

correct_answer.summary: The Cyber & Systems Security concentration (within the 4-year Computer Science track) requires 155.0 total credits, of which 37 credits are faculty electives.
key_facts: Total credits required: 155.0
Faculty electives: 37 credits total
Core courses: 8 credits, at least 3 courses
Faculty elective breakdown includes 12 additional credits from List A (all CS faculty courses)
Faculty elective breakdown includes 9 additional credits from List A or List B (extra-faculty)

cases.evaluation_notes: The agent must give both the total credit figure (155.0) and the faculty-elective figure (37) specifically — a common failure mode is reporting only one of the two numbers, or reporting the core-course credit count (8) instead of the elective total.

cases.user_request: My cumulative average dropped to 63 this semester, but my course success rate is 70%. Am I in non-regular academic standing, and why?

correct_answer.summary: Yes, this student is in Non-Regular Academic Standing, because a weighted cumulative average below 65 is on its own sufficient to trigger it, and 63 is below that threshold — even though the 70% success rate is above the separate 66% success-rate threshold and does not itself trigger non-regular standing.
key_facts: Non-Regular Academic Standing is triggered if any single one of several conditions applies, not all of them
Condition: weighted cumulative average below 65 (this student's 63 meets this condition)
Separate condition: course success rate below 66% (this student's 70% does NOT meet this condition)
The student is in Non-Regular Academic Standing because of the average alone
Source regulation: Section 5.6 / תקנה 3.1.5

cases.evaluation_notes: Tests whether the agent understands the 'any one condition' (OR, not AND) structure of this regulation. A wrong answer might incorrectly conclude the student is fine because their success rate looks acceptable, missing that the average alone is disqualifying.

cases.user_request: How many physical education credits do I need to graduate from a Technion B.Sc., and is there a maximum I can take per semester?

correct_answer.summary: Technion B.Sc. students must complete 2 mandatory physical education credits, with a maximum of 1.5 credits allowed per semester. A medical exemption grants a full exemption with no replacement credits required.
key_facts: Mandatory physical education credits: 2 credits total
Maximum physical education credits per semester: 1.5
Medical exemption: fully exempt, no replacement credits required

cases.evaluation_notes: Both the total (2) and the per-semester cap (1.5) must appear — a common failure mode is giving only one of the two numbers, or omitting the medical-exemption detail entirely.

cases.user_request: I've completed 80 credits toward my 155-credit degree, and I'm interested in also pursuing a second degree that requires 120 credits. Can I apply now, and what would the minimum combined credit requirement be for the additional degree?

correct_answer.summary: Yes, this student can apply now — a dual/additional degree application requires at least 72 accumulated credits, and 80 exceeds that. The minimum combined credit requirement for the additional degree is 0.75 times the sum of the credits required by each track: 0.75 × (155 + 120) = 206.25 credits. The additional degree is only granted after completing the primary degree, with a supplementary-plan average of at least 65.
key_facts: Minimum credits to apply for a dual/additional degree: 72 credits accumulated
This student's 80 credits exceeds the 72-credit threshold, so they may apply
Minimum combined credits formula: 0.75 × (sum of credits required by each track)
Applying the formula: 0.75 × (155 + 120) = 206.25 credits
The additional degree is only granted after completing the primary degree
Supplementary-plan average must be at least 65

cases.evaluation_notes: Requires combining two separate facts (the 72-credit eligibility threshold and the 0.75x combined-credit formula) and doing the arithmetic (206.25) correctly — a genuine multi-hop test. A common failure mode is stating the formula but not computing the actual number, or forgetting the primary-degree-completion precondition.

cases.user_request: I'm finishing my Technion B.Sc. and want to apply for an MSc research track. What GPA do I need, and does it matter which institution my bachelor's is from?

correct_answer.summary: A Technion B.Sc. graduate applying for an MSc research track generally needs a minimum weighted average of 75, though individual academic units may set a higher threshold. Applicants from other, equivalent institutions are held to an equivalent standing, decided by the unit. An applicant with a GPA above 75 but below a specific unit's own threshold may still be recommended with detailed justification if they graduated at least 3 years earlier.
key_facts: Minimum GPA for Technion B.Sc. graduates applying to MSc: 75 weighted average
Individual academic units may set a higher threshold than 75
Applicants from equivalent (non-Technion) institutions: equivalent standing, decided by the unit
Exception: GPA above 75 but below a unit's threshold may still be recommended with detailed justification if the applicant graduated at least 3 years earlier
Source regulation: sec. 23.02

cases.evaluation_notes: The agent should give the 75 GPA figure specifically for Technion graduates and should not conflate it with the PhD threshold (80) — a common failure mode is answering with the PhD GPA requirement instead.

cases.user_request: What GPA do I need to graduate cum laude or summa cum laude from a Technion B.Sc. program?

correct_answer.summary: Summa cum laude requires a degree-list average of at least 91 (and standing above the channel threshold), awarded to roughly the top 3% per channel. Cum laude requires a degree-list average of at least 84 (also above the channel threshold), awarded to roughly the top 15% per channel, a group that includes the summa cum laude graduates.
key_facts: Summa cum laude: degree-list average of 91 or higher
Summa cum laude: awarded to approximately the top 3% of graduates per channel
Cum laude: degree-list average of 84 or higher
Cum laude: awarded to approximately the top 15% of graduates per channel (including summa cum laude recipients)
Both honors also require standing above the channel-specific threshold
Source regulation: Section 'Honors at Graduation' / תקנה 3.2.4

cases.evaluation_notes: Both GPA thresholds (91 and 84) must appear correctly assigned to the right honor level — a common failure mode is swapping the two, or citing only one of them.
